'''Greg Johnson''' (born 20 February 1990) is a [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=25 October 2019}}</ref>

He played for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]] and [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] in the [[2013 RFL Championship|Championship]]. He was contracted to the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League|top flight]], and spent time on loan from the [[Huddersfield Giants|Giants]] at the [[Batley Bulldogs|Bulldogs]] in the [[2011 RFL Championship|second tier]].

==Background==
Greg Johnson was born in [[Huddersfield]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
A [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] international,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.examiner.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/jamaica-international-greg-johnson-looking-4982810 |title=Jamaica international Greg Johnson looking to extend Huddersfield Giants stay|publisher=The Huddersfield Daily Examiner |date=6 Jan 2011|accessdate=12 July 2013}}</ref> Johnson made his Super League début for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in 2011 and amassed a total of 2 tries in 12 appearances.<ref name="loverugbyleague">{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/Greg-Johnson|title=Greg Johnson Player Stats|publisher=loverugbyleague.com|accessdate=2014-05-29}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk|date=5 Oct 2011|accessdate=3 Jan 2021|title=Former Huddersfield Giants player Greg Johnson jailed for 2 years over pub attack: Full story|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/news/west-yorkshire-news/former-huddersfield-giants-player-greg-4965494}}</ref> His contract with Wakefield was immediately terminated following his conviction.<ref name="bbc">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-england-leeds-15172151|title=BBC News - Wildcats rugby player Greg Johnson jailed for bar attack|work=BBC News |date=4 October 2011 |accessdate=2014-05-29}}</ref>

When released from prison in 2013, he was signed by the [[Batley Bulldogs]], where he played regularly. He scored 18 tries in 28 appearances and attracted interest from the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], with whom he signed for the 2014 season.<ref name="thereddevils">{{cite web|url=http://www.thereddevils.net/news/rugby-news/welcome-aboard-greg-johnson|title=WELCOME ABOARD – GREG JOHNSON | The Red Devils RLFC|date=22 November 2013 |publisher=thereddevils.net|accessdate=2014-05-29}}</ref>

Johnson established himself as a first-choice player for Salford in 2014, scoring 10 tries in 20 appearances.<ref name="loverugbyleague"/> His first try of the season against the [[Widnes Vikings]] has been claimed to be a try of the season contender, as he bust three tackles in a run from his own half before sidestepping [[Rhys Hanbury]] to score.<ref name="dailystar2">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailystar.co.uk/rugby-league/368544/Widnes-32-Salford-18-Greg-Johnson-s-wonderful-try-not-enough-as-Widnes-take-the-points|title=Widnes 32 - Salford 18: Greg Johnson's wonderful try not enough as Widnes take the points | Rugby League | Sport | Daily Star. Simply The Best 7 Days A Week|date=7 March 2014 |publisher=[[dailystar.co.uk]]|accessdate=2014-05-29}}</ref>{{deps|certain=y|date=March 2023}}

===Bradford Bulls===
Johnson was released by the [[Bradford Bulls]] in September 2020.

===Batley Bulldogs===
On 2 Jul 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=2 Jul 2021|accessdate=4 Jul 2021|title=Jamaica international Greg Johnson joins Batley|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jamaica-international-greg-johnson-joins-batley/}}</ref>
